565→ # Deduplicate by name - GitHub returns multiple runs per check name 566→ # from reruns/superseded workflows. 1076→class TestFetchCheckRunsDeduplication: 1077→ """Test that _fetch_check_runs deduplicates by check name.""" 1078→ 1079→ @patch("erk_statusline.statusline.subprocess.run") 1080→ def test_keeps_most_recent_run_per_name(self, mock_run: MagicMock) -> None: 1081→ """Two runs with same name, different conclusions - keeps first (most recent).""" 1082→ check_runs_response = { 1083→ "check_runs": [ 1084→ {"name": "ci/test", "status": "completed", "conclusion": "success"}, 1085→ {"name": "ci/test", "status": "completed", "conclusion": "cancelled"}, 1086→ ] 1087→ } 1088→ mock_run.return_value = MagicMock(returncode=0, stdout=json.dumps(check_runs_response)) 1089→ 1090→ result = _fetch_check_runs( 1091→ owner="owner", repo="repo", ref="sha123", cwd="/cwd", timeout=1.5 1092→ ) 1093→ 1094→ assert len(result) == 1 1095→ assert result[0]["name"] == "ci/test" 1096→ assert result[0]["conclusion"] == "SUCCESS" 1097→ 1098→ @patch("erk_statusline.statusline.subprocess.run") 1099→ def test_deduplicates_with_mixed_unique_and_duplicate(self, mock_run: MagicMock) -> None: 1100→ """Three runs: two dupes + one unique - returns 2 check contexts.""" 1101→ check_runs_response = { 1102→ "check_runs": [ 1103→ {"name": "ci/test", "status": "completed", "conclusion": "success"}, 1104→ {"name": "ci/lint", "status": "completed", "conclusion": "success"}, 1105→ {"name": "ci/test", "status": "completed", "conclusion": "failure"},

session.assistant:
Perfect! I found both sections. Here are the findings:
## Findings
### 1. Deduplication Algorithm (lines 565-574)
The exact dedup algorithm in `_fetch_check_runs()`:
```python # Deduplicate by name - GitHub returns multiple runs per check name # from reruns/superseded workflows. Keep first (most recent) per name. seen_names: set[str] = set() deduplicated: list[dict[str, str]] = [] for ctx in check_contexts: name = ctx.get("name", "") if name not in seen_names: seen_names.add(name) deduplicated.append(ctx) check_contexts = deduplicated ```
**Algorithm**: Maintains an insertion-ordered dedupe using a set of seen names. Keeps the first occurrence of each check name (most recent) and discards subsequent duplicates.
### 2. Deduplication Test Cases (lines 1076-1135)
There are **3 test cases** covering deduplication in `TestFetchCheckRunsDeduplication`:
1. **`test_keeps_most_recent_run_per_name`** (lines 1080-1096) — Two runs with same name but different conclusions; verifies first (most recent) is kept 2. **`test_deduplicates_with_mixed_unique_and_duplicate`** (lines 1099-1117) — Three runs with mixed duplicates and unique names; verifies 2 distinct check names returned 3. **`test_all_unique_names_unchanged`** (lines 1120-1135) — Three runs all with unique names; verifies no dedup occurs (all 3 returned)
**File paths:** - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-statusline/src/erk_statusline/statusline.py` (lines 565-574) - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-statusline/tests/test_statusline.py` (lines 1076-1135)
